event: erythematous and edematous right lower extremity
suffered a laceration to the affected leg
while boating on a freshwater river in Michigan, USA
remained in wet pants for multiple hours
increased edema, erythema, and pain to the affected leg
vital signs were within normal limits
admitted to the hospital for cellulitis of the right lower extremity, bandemia, and lactic acidosis
discovered to have acute hypoxic respiratory failure
transferred to the Intensive Care Unit secondary to severe sepsis
started cefepime and metronidazole in the ICU
antimicrobial susceptibilities displayed the organism was sensitive to ceftriaxone, ciprofloxacin, ertapenem, and meropenem
added ciprofloxacin to her treatment regimen
discontinued ciprofloxacin after 7 days due to concerns for Clostridium difficile infection
general surgery was consulted and performed a wound debridement on the seventh day
condition continued to improve throughout her 14-day hospital stay
discharged to inpatient rehab on a 10-day course of 500 mg metronidazole TID and a one month course of 2 g ceftriaxone IVPB administered daily
discharged from inpatient rehab after an additional 14 days
instructions to follow up with her family doctor
